name: Suspicious CLR Logs File Creation
description: Detects the creation of CLR usage logs by suspicious processes. .NET code execution via applications like mshta, cscript, wscript, regsvr32, and wmic is often used by adversaries to execute malicious scripts. This rule identifies a specific file creation pattern associated with this activity.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the file creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for FileCreate (11).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the suspicious process creating the CLR log file (mshta.exe, cscript.exe, wscript.exe, regsvr32.exe, or wmic.exe).

parameters.name: target_filename
parameters.description: The full path to the created CLR usage log file (e.g., 'C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Microsoft\CLR*\UsageLogs\*').

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
